projects.title: Venture Capital (VC) Experience and Start-Up Funding Performance (2015)
projects.description: Our research looks to assess the relationship between founders’ previous venture capitalist experience and the effect this had on the time taken (quickness) to obtain investment. In order to do this, we gathered data regarding founders’ venture capital experience, previous start-up founding experience, and start-up founding date (if it was founded pre or post recession) on all current Boston software start-ups who successfully procured two major initial funding rounds (Series A and B). We find that previous venture capital experience did not have an effect on funding quickness. In fact, what did have a positive statistically significant impact is the number of previous start-ups founded. While founders’ previous venture capital experience has no impact, these results indicate that the potential success of gathering funding for technology start-ups in the Boston community is dependent on the founders’ previous start-ups founded and when the start-up was founded (pre or post recession).
projects.associatedWith: Associated with The London School of Economics and Political Science (LSE)

receivedRecommendations.givenByHeadline: Product Management Leader for Cloud and Network
receivedRecommendations.description: Lisa is energetic and creative.  She quickly learned to navigate a large company to provide leadership.  She owned the optimization of a large development project and steered direction on a new strategic product offering.  great team player.

receivedRecommendations.givenByHeadline: Directeur de l’Institut d’Economie de l’Eau | Auteur de « La fin de l’eau ? » (Fayard)
receivedRecommendations.description: Lisa was my student for two terms in the core course MG202– Analytical Methods for Management – while I was lecturing at the LSE in 2014-2015. Lisa is among the most brilliant student I ever had: she was always over-prepared during the seminars and keen on having feedbacks to improve her work. Apart her pure academic skills, Lisa showed strong communication skills inside and outside the classroom. On a more personal note, I felt that Lisa was strongly appreciated by both her peers and professors for her outgoing, warm and supportive attitude. I warmly support her in her future applications and career.
